text: When fanning out read-only reviewers in parallel with a mutating tester over shared state files, warn EVERY reader about transient mutations (or sequence them) — otherwise reviewers report probe-injected state as live findings.

graphify-out/ is in .gitignore but files committed before the rule (graph.json, manifest.json, GRAPH_REPORT.md, graph.html, dated snapshots) remain tracked and churn porcelain/auto-commits on every rebuild — treat those M entries as ambient, and don't trust the "graphify-out is gitignored" doc claim for tracked paths.

Extension messages sent with deliverAs:"nextTurn" (graph-first nudge, impact-trace) are invisible to the agent within the same run — verify those branches behaviorally (e.g. the 2nd structure grep blocking proves the 1st nudged) or by jiti-loading the extension with a stub pi that records sendMessage.

In ~/.pi/agent's graphify graph, ~41% of nodes are the vendored git/github.com/DietrichGebert/ponytail repo whose only bridge to the config-repo is a shared `path` import, so a FULL code re-extraction (/graph update or bare graphify) can detach it and drop the giant-component fraction to ~0.48, re-tripping the audit-pipelines ratchet even from a healthy graph — restore from graphify-out/<date>/ an

In ~/.pi/agent's graphify graph, even an incremental `--update` that re-extracts code files (AST-only, no semantic pass) collapses the giant-component fraction (observed 0.669→0.31) because build_merge's replace-on-re-extract drops those files' prior semantic/INFERRED bridge edges without regenerating them — always run the post-build giant-component guard and restore from the pre-run backup if it

When rebuilding the ~/.pi/agent graphify graph, Gemini extract_corpus_parallel at the default 60k token_budget yields too-sparse semantic edges (giant fraction ~0.2-0.3); use token_budget≈12000 + deep_mode=True and then add deterministic INFERRED 'mentions' bridge edges (doc/code text containing another file's relpath or unique basename, mapped via make_id of relpath-sans-ext) to restore giant-com

The graphify semantic cache lives inside graphify-out/cache, so deleting graphify-out forces a full paid LLM re-extraction — before rebuilding, check whether the old graph.json is recoverable from a pre-untrack git commit (git show <sha>:graphify-out/graph.json).

When acting on a scout's duplication audit, independently grep the whole directory for the shared pattern before treating the enumerated list as complete — the scout's "7 scripts" jiti-bootstrap list missed test-config-paths.mjs which shared the exact idiom.
